2604.15951.title: Integrating Graphs, Large Language Models, and Agents: Reasoning and Retrieval
2604.15951.paper_summary: Generative AI, particularly Large Language Models, increasingly integrates graph-based representations to enhance reasoning, retrieval, and structured decision-making. Despite rapid advances, there remains limited clarity regarding when, why, where, and what types of graph-LLM integrations are most appropriate across applications. This survey provides a concise, structured overview of the design choices underlying the integration of graphs with LLMs. We categorize existing methods based on their purpose (reasoning, retrieval, generation, recommendation), graph modality (knowledge graphs, scene graphs, interaction graphs, causal graphs, dependency graphs), and integration strategies (prompting, augmentation, training, or agent-based use). By mapping representative works across domains such as cybersecurity, healthcare, materials science, finance, robotics, and multimodal environments, we highlight the strengths, limitations, and best-fit scenarios for each technique. This survey aims to offer researchers a practical guide for selecting the most suitable graph-LLM approach depending on task requirements, data characteristics, and reasoning complexity.
2604.15951.paper_summary_zh: 生成式人工智慧，特別是大型語言模型，越來越多地整合基於圖的表示，以增強推理、檢索和結構化決策。儘管快速進展，對於何時、為何、在哪裡以及哪些類型的圖-LLM 整合在各種應用中最為合適，仍然缺乏明確的認識。此調查提供了圖與 LLM 整合設計選擇的簡明、結構化概述。我們根據其目的（推理、檢索、生成、推薦）、圖的類型（知識圖、場景圖、互動圖、因果圖、依賴圖）和整合策略（提示、增強、訓練或基於代理的使用）對現有方法進行分類。通過映射網絡安全、醫療保健、材料科學、金融、機器人技術和多模態環境等領域的代表性作品，我們突顯每種技術的優勢、限制和最佳適用情境。此調查旨在為研究人員提供一個實用指南，以根據任務要求、數據特徵和推理複雜性選擇最合適的圖-LLM 方法。
2604.15951.author: Hamed Jelodar et.al.
2604.15951.authors: Hamed Jelodar, Samita Bai, Mohammad Meymani, Parisa Hamedi, Roozbeh Razavi-Far, Ali Ghorbani
